CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-16-2023 Set For Trial On Sep-23-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-01-2023 Continued To Nov-29-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-31-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CHRISTOPHER ROSE DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Defendant Christopher Rose's demurrer to the second cause of action in the complaint is SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. A cause of action for fraud "must be pled specifically; general and conclusory allegations do not suffice." (Lazar v. Superior Court (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645; Hamilton v. Greenwich Investors XXVI, LLC (2011) 195 Cal.App.4th 1602, 1614.) To satisfy "'[t]his particularity requirement,'" a plaintiff must plead facts that show "how, when, where, to whom, and by what means the representations were tendered." (Lazar, supra, 12 Cal.4th at 645.) Here, plaintiff does not meet the heightened pleading requirement. For example, Paragraph 19 does not disclose the how (e.g., oral, text, email), the when or the where of the alleged misrepresentations.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
